GlobalTech Corp (incorporated in Delaware) sells electronics online nationwide and operates a regional office in Illinois. Michael, a citizen of Illinois, sues GlobalTech Corp in Illinois. Does the court have personal jurisdiction?

<lane>L01</lane>
<tool_call>{"tool": "z3", "method": "check_elements", "elements": ["minimum_contacts", "purposeful_availment", "fair_play_and_substantial_justice"], "facts": ["GlobalTech Corp is incorporated in Delaware", "sells electronics online nationwide and operates a regional office in Illinois", "Plaintiff Michael is a citizen of Illinois"], "matching": {"minimum_contacts": true, "purposeful_availment": true, "fair_play_and_substantial_justice": true}, "mode": "and"}</tool_call>
{"all_met": true}

Yes. GlobalTech Corp's nationwide sales operations, regional office in Illinois, and deliberate targeting of Illinois consumers establish minimum contacts and purposeful availment. Exercising jurisdiction is consistent with fair play and substantial justice. Personal jurisdiction is proper.
